title: Convergence Report
summary: The ECB's June 2025 Convergence Report assesses Bulgaria's progress toward euro adoption, finding that Bulgaria has made good progress towards economic convergence and that Bulgarian law is compatible with EU Treaties and ECB Statute requirements under Article 131. The report concludes that Bulgaria is within the reference values of the convergence criteria and complies with the legal requirements.
key_points: Bulgaria has made good progress towards economic convergence with the euro area since the previous report in 2024
Bulgarian law is compatible with the Treaties and the Protocol on the Statute of the European System of Central Banks and of the European Central Bank
Overall, Bulgaria is within the reference values of the convergence criteria and complies with the legal requirements
The Convergence Report is published at least once every two years, or at the request of an EU Member State
All EU Member States except Denmark are required to adopt the euro upon meeting convergence criteria
Croatia adopted the euro on 1 January 2023, becoming the 20th member of the euro area
why_it_matters: This Convergence Report represents an official ECB assessment of Bulgaria's readiness to join the euro area. A positive assessment could lead to Bulgaria adopting the euro, expanding the monetary union. For currency traders and fixed income investors, euro area enlargement has implications for euro liquidity, ECB governance composition, and EU fiscal policy coordination. This is relevant for macro-driven FX and sovereign bond positioning.
macro_context: The Convergence Report is a precondition for euro adoption under EU treaties. Bulgaria meeting convergence criteria signals potential future euro area expansion. The report covers five convergence criteria: price stability, sound public finances, exchange rate stability, long-term interest rate convergence, and legal convergence. Successful euro adoption would integrate Bulgaria into the common monetary policy framework.

issues_found: Source is a listing/index page (index.en.html), not the actual report content
Full report PDF required for substantive analysis
Record source_type correctly identifies listing_page - Rule 5 requires rejection

notes: This source is a landing page index for ECB Convergence Reports rather than the full report content. The page summarizes that the June 2025 report found Bulgaria compliant with convergence criteria. Actual data points (reference values, inflation rates, debt levels, etc.) would be in the PDF. This is tangentially relevant to macro catalysts as euro area expansion is a structural macro event, but the source itself contains minimal actionable data. Published in June 2025, making it recent and not stale.

reasoning: Landing page/navigation index provides only summary content
